input.prompt:
I'm worried these tests might have problems. Audit
`Payments.Tests/PaymentProcessorTests.cs` for .NET test anti-patterns
using the full anti-pattern catalog (missing assertions, swallowed
exceptions, always-true/self-comparing assertions, flakiness, magic
values, duplicate tests, broad exception types, etc.) and tell me
what (if anything) genuinely needs fixing — be careful not to invent
issues that aren't really there.

Give me a severity-ranked list (Critical / Warning / Info / "no
issues found"). Each finding must be tied to a named anti-pattern
from the catalog and include a concrete code-level fix. Do not edit
the files — this is a read-only review.

data.reasoningText: The user wants me to audit a test file for .NET test anti-patterns. Let me read the file first.

data.content:
## Audit Result: **No Issues Found** ✅

This test class is clean. Here's why none of the common anti-patterns apply:

| Anti-Pattern | Verdict |
|---|---|
| Missing assertions | ❌ Every test has explicit asserts on the outcome |
| Swallowed exceptions | ❌ `ThrowsException<T>` properly validates expected throws |
| Always-true / self-comparing assertions | ❌ All asserts compare result against distinct expected values |
| Broad exception types | ❌ Tests assert the specific `ArgumentOutOfRangeException`, not `Exception` |
| Magic values | ❌ Amounts/order-IDs are simple inline literals appropriate for unit tests |
| Duplicate tests | ❌ Zero/negative amount tests cover distinct boundary conditions |
| Flakiness | ❌ No shared state, no async races, deterministic `FakeGateway` |
| Test logic in production code | ❌ Uses a test double (`FakeGateway`) injected via constructor |

The tests follow Arrange-Act-Assert, use parameterized `[DataRow]` correctly, assert precise exception types, and verify both happy and sad paths. Nothing here warrants a change.
